steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to specify oxygen's total mass relative to all elements on Earth. A mass fraction is defined in the notes as that exact ratio, and oxygen's mass fraction is 297,000 ppm. The correct choice must state that value.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Oxygen's abundance on Earth is greater than vanadium's, as reflected by oxygen's higher mass fraction.: compares oxygen to vanadium rather than to all elements; doesn't specify oxygen's mass fraction value. ✗
  • Choice B — Oxygen's abundance can be expressed by a mass fraction, a measure of the element's mass relative to that of all elements on Earth.: defines the concept but omits the specific 297,000 ppm value. ✗
  • Choice C — The mass fraction of oxygen on Earth is 297,000 ppm.: states the exact mass fraction, which by definition is the ratio of oxygen's mass to the combined mass of all elements. ✓
  • Choice D — Oxygen's mass relative to the combined mass of all elements on Earth is 105 ppm.: uses vanadium's value (105 ppm) for oxygen — factually wrong. ✗
C is correct.
